title: 3 Legal and Policy Considerations for Business Taxes
body_text:
3	Legal and Policy Considerations for Business Taxes
3.1	CPA Rules
CPA Rule G4, Canada Revenue Agency Acceptance of Payments Goods and Services Tax/Harmonized Sales Tax (GST/HST), Excise, Taxation, and Customs Programs, provides information that pertains to accepting and processing payments for the GST/HST, Excise, Taxation, and Customs programs of Canada Revenue Agency (CRA). Considerations include the following:
Payments received outside of regular business hours must be treated as the next dayâs business and should be date-stamped accordingly.
A remittance voucher can only be accepted if a payment is made by the presenter.
Both parts of the remittance voucher must be stamped with the financial institutionâs date stamp.
If a cheque is accepted in payment of a GST/HST, Excise, Taxation, or Customs remittance and the cheque is dishonoured by the Drawee financial institution, attempt to recover funds. If funds arenât recovered, request reimbursement from the federal government.
For further details, refer to the CPA Rule G4 â Canada Revenue Agency Acceptance of Payments Goods and Services Tax/Harmonized Sales Tax (GST/HST), Excise, Taxation, and Customs Programs.
3.2	Policy on Internal Controls for Business Taxes
Your financial institution is responsible for Business Taxes security practices and internal controls. For more information, see the
PaymentStream
â¢
Direct Part.
3.3	Policy on Handling Processed Remittance Vouchers
Your financial institution is responsible for setting up a policy on how to handle remittance vouchers that have been processed in Business Taxes. CRA doesnât mandate whether the paper remittance vouchers should be archived or destroyed. Ensure they are destroyed in a secure manner.
END
